contents.value: **@interface** — Objective-C keyword.
**@implementation** — Objective-C keyword.
**@end** — Objective-C keyword.
**@protocol** — Objective-C keyword.
**@property** — Objective-C keyword.
**NSLog** — Built-in function/command in Objective-C.
**alloc** — Built-in function/command in Objective-C.
**init** — Built-in function/command in Objective-C.
**dealloc** — Built-in function/command in Objective-C.
**copy** — Built-in function/command in Objective-C.

A **pointer** stores the memory address of another variable.
```c
int *ptr = &value;
```
A **struct** groups related data fields into a single composite type.
An **enum** defines a type with a fixed set of named constants.
A **union** stores different data types in the same memory location.

**typedef** creates an alias for an existing type.
```c
typedef unsigned long ulong;
```

**malloc** allocates a block of memory on the heap and returns a pointer.
```c
int *p = malloc(n * sizeof(int));
```

**free** releases previously allocated heap memory.
```c
free(ptr);
```
**sizeof** returns the size in bytes of a type or variable.
**volatile** tells the compiler a variable may change unexpectedly (e.g., hardware register).
**static** limits scope to the current file/function, or preserves value between calls.
**extern** declares a variable or function defined in another translation unit.
**inline** suggests the compiler insert the function body at the call site.
**const** marks a value as read-only after initialization.
**register** hints the compiler to store the variable in a CPU register.

**Preprocessor directives** run before compilation.
```c
#include, #define, #ifdef, #pragma
```
